reported_entities.name: Xiaomi
reported_entities.claim: Xiaomi confirmed to use MediaTek Dimensity chipsets across various product lines, including flagship models.

reported_entities.name: Redmi
reported_entities.claim: Redmi (Xiaomi sub-brand) ships Dimensity-powered devices, e.g. Redmi Note 13 Pro+ using MediaTek Dimensity 7200-Ultra.

reported_entities.name: POCO
reported_entities.claim: POCO (Xiaomi sub-brand) has released MediaTek Dimensity devices, e.g. Poco X6 Pro using Dimensity 8300-Ultra.

reported_entities.name: Vivo
reported_entities.claim: Vivo confirmed to use MediaTek Dimensity chipset, particularly for flagship models.

reported_entities.name: iQOO
reported_entities.claim: iQOO (Vivo sub-brand) offers phones with MediaTek Dimensity chipsets onboard.

reported_entities.name: Oppo
reported_entities.claim: Oppo confirmed to use MediaTek Dimensity chipset, particularly for flagship models; also noted as a global brand choosing Dimensity chips.

reported_entities.name: Realme
reported_entities.claim: Realme listed among global brands for which MediaTek Dimensity chips are the choice.

reported_entities.name: OnePlus
reported_entities.claim: OnePlus confirmed to use Dimensity chipset for flagship models; OnePlus Nord 2 (2021) used Dimensity 1200, marking MediaTek's entry into the brand.

reported_entities.name: Samsung
reported_entities.claim: Samsung listed among global brands for which MediaTek Dimensity chips are the choice, for some models sold in certain regions.

reported_entities.name: Motorola
reported_entities.claim: Motorola among brands confirmed using MediaTek Dimensity processors.

reported_entities.name: Nokia
reported_entities.claim: Nokia listed among global brands for which MediaTek Dimensity chips are the choice.

reported_entities.name: Huawei
reported_entities.claim: Huawei confirmed to use the Dimensity chipset, particularly for flagship models.

reported_entities.name: LG
reported_entities.claim: LG listed among global brands for which MediaTek Dimensity chips are the choice.

reported_entities.name: Honor
reported_entities.claim: Honor has released phones with MediaTek Dimensity processors.

reported_entities.name: Tecno
reported_entities.claim: Tecno uses MediaTek processors in upper-midrange and performance-focused devices, including Dimensity models.

reported_entities.name: Infinix
reported_entities.claim: Infinix has released phones with MediaTek Dimensity processors.

reported_entities.name: Asus
reported_entities.claim: Asus has released phones with MediaTek Dimensity processors.

notes: Salva discovery run (objective=find_partnership_signals, industry=smartphone/mobile devices, product=chipset, keywords incl. 'MediaTek Dimensity') completed with retrieval_health=ok but qualified_count=0 — all 5 raw candidates across 2 rounds were filtered out as low_signal/untrusted_source (noise domains: en.wikipedia.org, www.intel.com, www.amd.com, www.mediatek.com, www.hp.com); no entities survived qualification. Fell back to 2 WebSearch queries to identify brands. Findings aggregate across multiple secondary sources (91mobiles, Beebom, MediaTek's own i.mediatek.com/mediatek-5g page, Techarc, WonderfulPCB) rather than a single authoritative MediaTek partner list page; MediaTek does not appear to publish one consolidated official partner roster. Note some entries are sub-brands of a parent (Redmi/POCO under Xiaomi; iQOO under Vivo) — listed separately since sources treat them as distinct device brands.
